steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Label the side pair for the named angle first: opposite, adjacent, and hypotenuse. Then pick the matching shortcut, such as SOH/CAH/TOA, complementary trig values, or a special right-triangle ratio.

steps.title: Recognize complementary angles in the right triangle
steps.content: In right triangle JKL, we're told that the sum of angles J and K equals $90^{\circ}$. This means J and K are complementary angles - together they make a right angle.

steps.title: Apply the cofunction identity
steps.content:
Since $J + K = 90^{\circ}$, we can write $K = 90^{\circ} - J$. Therefore:
$$\cos(K) = \cos(90^{\circ} - J) = \sin(J)$$

steps.title: Substitute the given value and identify the answer
steps.content:
We're given that $\sin(J) = \frac{\sqrt{19}}{14}$.
By the cofunction identity from Step 2: $$\cos(K) = \sin(J) = \frac{\sqrt{19}}{14}$$ This matches.
